title: gcp-sm put: zeroization gap present but not documented (unlike get)
description: gcp-sm::get() has an explicit ZEROIZATION GAP comment explaining that reqwest/.json()/serde_json leaves secret material in non-Zeroizing allocations. The same gap exists in put(): base64::encode() produces a plain String, serde_json::json!() builds a non-Zeroizing body Value containing the encoded secret. There is no equivalent comment in put(). Scenario: a contributor reads the get() comment, addresses it there, and ships a PR that fixes get but leaves put with the same gap — the missing comment makes the omission invisible. Maintenance consequence: partial fix shipped as complete fix.
